summary: Package silently exfiltrates console output and caller data to attacker-controlled Telegram and Firebase endpoints on require
details: On require, index.js loads 66o.js which replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy that intercepts every `console.log`, `console.error`, and `console.dir` call and transmits the stringified arguments to hardcoded Telegram chat IDs via a hardcoded bot token, as well as PUTting `console.dir` output to `https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/...json`. This occurs automatically at import time without installer consent and captures any data the installer's application logs — tokens, PII, stack traces, secrets. Additionally, index.js installs `globalThis.E` with methods that PATCH arbitrary arguments to `https://i----i.firebaseio.com/<x>.json` and call Telegram bot API / Cloudinary upload preset `o6oooo` using a second hardcoded bot token. Supporting evasion signals: no README, no description, placeholder package.json, obfuscated host names (`i----i`, `iiilll`) and filenames (`66o.js`, `8oo`). This is a covert data-collection harness, not a legitimate library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
